The old plantation house loomed against the darkening sky, its shadow stretching like a corpse across the overgrown lawn. Twisted oaks dripped with Spanish moss, swaying in the windless dread of a Louisiana night.

Branner: Something evil walks these halls. I can feel it in my bones.

The silence was absolute, broken only by the distant cry of a night bird deep in the bayou swamp.

---

A sudden crash echoed through the corridor, and Griswell froze with his pistol raised. The tension was unbearable as he edged toward the half-open door, heart hammering against his ribs.

Griswell: Show yourself, damn you!

He burst into the room and found only shadows, the stench of blood thick in the humid air. A dark stain spread across the ancient floorboards.

---

Mystery shrouded the old ruined staircase where strange markings had been carved by unknown hands long ago. The hidden passage vanished into darkness beyond the lantern's reach.

Branner: These symbols are ancient. No living man made them.

Griswell felt the peculiar dread of something watching from the bizarre shadows, something that had haunted this place since before memory began.
